headline: Hooks now read permission lists from settings.json directly

bullets: approve-safe-commands and auto-mode-shared-steps now mirror your settings.json permissions directly — edit settings.json, hook behavior follows. No drift possible.
New shared lib lib/settings-permissions.sh — load once at hook source-time, pure-bash regex match after.
Removed validate-safe-commands-sync.sh — it policed drift that can no longer occur.

settings.template.json permissions.ask now lists 9 gh subcommands the auto-mode hook already gated via a hardcoded cascade (gh issue {transfer,pin,unpin,lock,unlock}, gh release download, gh repo {unarchive,sync,deploy-key}). No behavior change for existing projects; new projects' settings.json reflects the explicit list on first sync.
